title: Jaylen Brown sheds light on 1-on-1 meeting with Jayson Tatum ahead of playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum held a meeting to improve communication and set a tone for the Celtics' playoff push.
Jayson Tatum returned from a 10-month Achilles rehab, and the Celtics have won six of their last ten games.
The Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference; playoffs begin April 18th, following their game against the Knicks.

title: Livestream: Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial gets underway
author: John Waller
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, was shot by a colleague and now faces trial.
Fitzsimmons pleaded not guilty to assault with a dangerous weapon in the incident last summer.
The trial will be livestreamed by NBC10 Boston for public viewing.

title: How Dropkick Murphys’ Jeff DaRosa found himself on the road to the Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jeff DaRosa of Dropkick Murphys is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Claddagh Fund after getting sober.
DaRosa began running casually, inspired after performing at the Boston Strong benefit show following the 2013 bombings.
Turning 40 was a pivotal moment for DaRosa, leading to sobriety and a disciplined approach to marathon training.

title: Jayson Tatum, frustrated by shooting slump, is showing both rust and flashes
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum is experiencing a shooting slump after returning from a serious injury, shooting 38.8% from the field.
Despite his shooting struggles, Tatum is averaging 8.9 rebounds per game and increasing his playing time.
The Celtics won six of their first eight games since Tatum's return, maintaining second place in the Eastern Conference.

title: Bones Hyland, short-handed Timberwolves beat ice-cold Celtics: 6 takeaways
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics fall to Timberwolves 102-92, with Bones Hyland scoring a team-high 23 points.
Jayson Tatum struggled, shooting 6-for-16, reflecting a challenging return from injury rehab.
Timberwolves secured their first win in Boston since 2005, aided by a key fourth-quarter run.

title: Will President Trump send ICE agents to Boston’s Logan Airport?
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: President Trump proposes deploying ICE agents to airports, including Boston Logan, unless DHS funding is approved.
Logan Airport has not experienced significant disruptions and Massport has no information on ICE agents arriving.
TSA union inquiries ongoing; no official confirmation of ICE deployment to New England airports amid government shutdown.

title: Is Bruins goalie Jeremy Swayman a legitimate Vezina candidate? The numbers speak for themselves.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman made 42 saves to secure a 4-2 victory for the Bruins against the Red Wings.
Despite a .908 save percentage, Swayman ranks second in the NHL for goals saved above expected with 27.1.
Swayman's strong performances amidst team's defensive struggles make him a Vezina Trophy contender.

title: Celtics legend Paul Pierce continues to make a case for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce advocates for Jaylen Brown as a deserving candidate for NBA MVP honors this season.
Jaylen Brown averages career-highs with 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game, leading Celtics.
Pierce attributes lack of MVP talk for Celtics players to Boston's sustained sports success and team achievements.

title: Plymouth police officer on paid leave amid criminal investigation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Plymouth officer Samantha Pelrine on paid leave during State Police investigation into off-duty conduct.
Massachusetts State Police launched a criminal investigation last week; details remain undisclosed.
Plymouth Police conducting internal investigation, emphasizing policy violations won't be tolerated.

title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony, a Boston Red Sox outfielder, is predicted to win the AL MVP by MLB writer Eno Sarris.
Anthony impressed in 2025 with a .292 batting average and played for Team USA in the 2026 World Baseball Classic.
Despite a high ground-ball rate, Anthony's power-hitting potential and improved swing path are highlighted by Sarris.
